hero.question: Will Novak Djokovic be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
hero.prob_take: At 7.4%, the crowd has essentially priced Djokovic out, betting he won't even be competitive at Wimbledon 2026 when he'll be 39 years old with creeping injury concerns. The real question is whether 7.4% undervalues his historical dominance at the All England Club (three titles) and his proven ability to peak for majors, making this a contrarian value if he stays healthy through 2025.

daily_take.headline: Djokovic at 7.4% looks like free money if his knees hold up two years.
daily_take.deck: Novak's odds jumped 3.1 points on speculation he might skip the 2025 season to focus on 2026. The market is pricing in permanent decline, but it's overweighting one bad year against a career of comebacks. He'll be 38 at Wimbledon 2026, which is the real issue, not retirement rumors. Smart money waits for him to play next year before collapsing the odds further, or locks in 7.4% now if you believe his body has one more peak left.
